content.problemStatement:
Chef has planned a vacation with his friends in the month of March, and is really looking forward to it!

The vacation begins on the XXX-th of March.

As days get closer to the vacation date, Chef gets more and more excited for it.

Chef's excitement level starts at YYY on March 111. It will then increase by 111 for every day that passes; so it will be Y+1Y+1Y+1 on March 222-nd, Y+2Y+2Y+2 on March 333-rd, and so on.

What will Chef's excitement level be on the day the vacation begins?


Input Format

The only line of input will contain two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the date of the trip and Chef's excitement level on March 111-st, respectively.


Output Format
Output a single integer: Chef's excitement level on the day the vacation begins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The vacation begins on March third.
Chef's excitement starts at 555, then increases to 666 on March 222-nd and then to 777 on March 333-rd.
The answer is Chef's excitement on March 333-rd, which is 777.

Explanation:
The vacation begins on March 111-st; so the answer is just Chef's initial excitement, which is 888.

Explanation:
Chef's excitement starts at 666, and keeps increasing by 111 every day.
Finally, on March 272727, it will be 323232.
